data.description:
## Why
The landing action itself: wait for CI to re-verify the rebased tree (every chain member) and submit the tip atomically so the tested tree is exactly the merged tree (the R5 gate invariant). Split out of S2 (`perovskite-frilly-grebe`).

## Files
- `infra/autolander/loop.py` — fresh-Verified await + ancestor-atomic submit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Fresh Verified (per member):** a `Verified +1` on the exact rebased patchset SHA (not a copied/carried vote). **For a multi-member chain, `rebase:chain` mints a NEW patchset (distinct SHA) per member, so ALL members must carry a fresh `Verified +1` on their own post-rebase SHAs before submit** — submitting while any member lacks a fresh Verified would land an untested tree (violates R5).
- [ ] **Bounded await:** await fresh Verified up to a bounded timeout (default 30 min, configurable, matching `land`'s timeout); on timeout (CI hung), hand the stack back (S2b mechanism) rather than blocking forever. (`LLM-Review` carries `TRIVIAL_REBASE`/`MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` per ADR-0041; `Verified` re-runs on each new patchset.)
- [ ] **Ancestor-atomic submit** = a single `POST /changes/{tip-id}/submit` on the chain tip; under FFO with relation-chain semantics this submits the tip together with all its ancestors atomically (all-or-nothing), never a partial stack. On success, close/annotate the associated ticket(s) via `import rebar` ticket ops.
- [ ] **Tests:** unit-test the all-members-fresh rule (submit blocked if any member's SHA lacks a fresh Verified) and the bounded-await timeout → hand-back; a live check lands one real single-change stack end-to-end.
data.title: S2c Fresh-Verified-per-member await + ancestor-atomic submit
